title: Some One
content:
Some one came knocking
        At my wee, small door;
     Some one came knocking,
        I'm sure - sure - sure;
     I listened, I opened,
        I looked to left and right,
     But nought there was a-stirring
        In the still dark night;
     Only the busy beetle
        Tap-tapping in the wall,
     Only from the forest
        The screech-owl's call,
     Only the cricket whistling
        While the dewdrops fall,
     So I know not who came knocking,
        At all, at all, at all.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Walter De La Mare, titled "Some One", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
